behaviors.description: The clause extractor identifies all key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Agentic, because correctness depends on real model extraction quality in the behavior text ('identifies all key terms'). A deterministic stub would not expose missed/partial extraction. Pass 2 downgrade check: Integration is insufficient for this behavior because wiring tests can pass while true extraction recall fails. Pass 2 upgrade check: Workflow is not required; the behavior is about one reviewer, not a multi-agent chain. Rejection gate: higher Workflow was rejected because the behavior names only the clause extractor, not multiple agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real clause extractor model on a labeled contract set with gold key-term annotations (parties, dates, obligations, penalties), evaluate recall per category, and fail if any category drops below the acceptance threshold across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: The risk assessor flags language that would be problematic under the configured risk policy, even when the problematic phrasing is indirect or euphemistic.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Agentic, because the behavior targets semantic judgment quality ('indirect or euphemistic' problematic language), which depends on real model output. Pass 2 downgrade check: Integration with canned responses would hide the core failure (missed subtle risk language). Pass 2 upgrade check: Workflow is unnecessary since this behavior concerns one reviewer's judgment, not chained agentic stages. Rejection gate: higher Workflow rejected because behavior text scopes to the risk assessor only.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the real risk assessor on a curated benchmark containing direct and euphemistic risk phrasing under the configured policy, and assert detection performance (e.g., recall/precision threshold) on the euphemistic subset.

behaviors.description: The compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all applicable regulatory requirements for the configured jurisdiction.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Agentic, because correctness here is substantive compliance determination quality for jurisdictional rules, not just routing. Pass 2 downgrade check: Integration can verify configuration plumbing but not whether model decisions are actually correct. Pass 2 upgrade check: Workflow is not required; this behavior is scoped to a single checker. Rejection gate: higher Workflow rejected because no multi-step agent chain is specified in this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real compliance checker against jurisdiction-labeled contracts with known compliant/non-compliant outcomes and requirement-level labels, then fail when decision accuracy/coverage is below threshold for the configured jurisdiction.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (e.g., extractor says 'standard' but risk assessor says 'high risk'), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because the failure boundary is the handoff between reviewer outputs and merge logic (cross-module data combination/attribution). Pass 2 downgrade check: Unit is possible for pure merge logic, but this behavior explicitly requires reviewer-origin attribution under conflict, so module wiring/contract compatibility is part of the failure mode. Pass 2 upgrade check: System/Agentic/Workflow are not required by behavior text; it does not require real OS/network, nor model-quality dependence, nor chained real agentic steps. Rejection gate: higher System and Agentic were rejected because behavior is about deterministic merge preservation given conflicting inputs, not infrastructure or model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire reviewer output producers (fixture-based extractor/risk results) into the real merge module, submit conflicting assessments for the same clause, and assert merged output keeps both assessments with correct reviewer attribution and no overwrite.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — dispatching to all three reviewers, collecting results, merging into a unified report — completes correctly for a real contract without losing any individual reviewer's findings.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Workflow, because behavior explicitly requires the full pipeline across multiple AI reviewers plus merge ('dispatching to all three reviewers, collecting results, merging'). That is multiple agentic steps in one end-to-end flow. Pass 2 downgrade check: Agentic alone is insufficient because it can validate one model step but not cross-step collection/merge loss across all three reviewers. Integration with stubs could miss real multi-agent output interactions. Pass 2 upgrade check: no higher level exists beyond Workflow. Rejection gate: none (Workflow is top level).
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run on a real contract using all three real reviewers in parallel, capture each reviewer’s raw findings, then assert the unified report contains every finding from each reviewer (with attribution) and that pipeline completion status is successful.
